summary: Cache /notes as a single envelope (it's ID-less, like results-report) and rewrite `bundle`'s notes + recommendations parsing for the real shapes: notes = {notes:[{note,category}]} matched by the biomarker's category or a whole-word, distinctive-name prose mention with HTML-entity decode; recommendations = nested envelope joined by Quest code.
reason: bundle parsed notes as {body,text,biomarker} and recs as {title,body,biomarker} — neither matches reality, so both sections were empty. notes also never cached (no extractable ID). Whole-word + distinctive-name gating prevents a short biomarker name like 'Lead'/'Iron' from matching the English verb in note prose.
validated_outcome: Live: bundle Hematocrit returns 3 relevant Blood notes (entities decoded) + omega-3 food recs; bundle Lead falls back to category-only (no 'lead to'/'lead a' verb false-positives).
